activities_projects.title: Human Number Line
activities_projects.objective: Understand addition and subtraction of integers physically
steps: Draw a long number line on the floor from -10 to 10
Stand at zero and move forward for positive and backward for negative
Solve $3 + (-5)$ by walking the steps and noting the final position
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes movement on a number line for integer operations
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct positioning on the number line (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Integer Sign Rules Table
activities_projects.objective: Memorize the rules for multiplication and division
steps: Create a $2 	imes 2$ grid for multiplication signs
Fill in the results for $(+) 	imes (+)$, $(+) 	imes (-)$, $(-) 	imes (+)$, and $(-) 	imes (-)$
Decorate the chart and hang it in the study area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Memorizes the fundamental sign laws of integers
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the sign rules (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Colored Counter Addition
activities_projects.objective: Add integers using the cancellation method
steps: Represent $-4 + 6$ using 4 red and 6 blue buttons
Pair one red with one blue to make 'zero pairs'
Count the remaining buttons to find the sum
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand the concept of additive inverse and zero pairs
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct count of remaining counters (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Temperature Tracking
activities_projects.objective: Apply integers to real-world temperature changes
steps: Note down the morning and night temperatures of five cold cities
Calculate the difference (fall in temperature) using subtraction
Represent the drop as a negative integer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses integers to represent real-life rising and falling values

activities_projects.title: The Integer Ladder
steps: Draw a vertical number line representing a building with basement floors
If a lift starts at floor 2 and goes down 5 floors, where is it?
Show the calculation: $2 - 5 = -3$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes vertical applications of negative numbers

activities_projects.title: Multiplication Patterns
activities_projects.objective: Observe how patterns lead to negative results
steps: Write the table of 3 down to $3 	imes 0$
Continue the pattern to $3 	imes (-1), 3 	imes (-2)$
Observe how the product decreases by 3 each time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Derives the rules of negative multiplication through patterns
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical sequence of the number pattern (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Integer War Card Game
activities_projects.objective: Compare the value of integers quickly
steps: Two players flip a card at the same time
The player with the higher integer value wins the round
Record the scores using a tally mark system
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quickly compares positive and negative magnitudes
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the greater integer (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Properties Match-Up
steps: Write $a + b = b + a$ on one card and its name on another
Do the same for Associative and Distributive properties
Shuffle and match the property with its correct equation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the fundamental properties of integer operations
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of all property pairs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Zero Sum Game
activities_projects.objective: Find integers that add up to zero
steps: Write integers from -10 to 10 on slips
Pick a slip and find another slip that makes the sum zero
Write down the pairs (e.g., $-7, 7$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Reinforces the concept of additive identity and inverse
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the zero-sum pairs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Integer Story Problems
steps: Create five short stories (e.g., 'A bird dives 4m down, then climbs 2m')
Write the mathematical expression for each story
Solve the expression to find the final answer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Translates linguistic problems into mathematical operations
